frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RPE-driven value learning via midbrain dopamine signals

frameworks.name: belief state
frameworks.one_line: probability distribution over hidden states under sensory ambiguity

core_question: How are dopamine reward prediction errors and subsequent learning regulated under state uncertainty?
key_assumptions: midbrain dopamine neurons signal reward prediction errors
ambiguous sensory data are integrated as a probability distribution over hypothetical states
RL operates on belief states rather than observed states
manipulations: two trained states defined by different reward amounts
intermediate-sized rewards delivered in rare test trials
key_findings: dopamine activity is a non-monotonic function of reward size
dopamine response pattern matches RL models operating on belief states
dopamine response magnitude quantitatively predicts behavioral changes
interpretations: state inference plays a critical role in reinforcement learning
dopamine system represents belief states, not only observed states
